# Republic Studio: Complete GitHub Setup\n\n**Republic is developed in Production Waves. GitHub is Republic Studio.**\n\nThis document summarizes the complete GitHub restructuring around Production Waves.\n\n---\n\n## What Changed\n\n### Before\n- Development organized by \"Phases\"\n- GitHub used as code repository only\n- Documentation separate from issues\n- Unclear connection between work and strategy\n\n### After\n- Development organized by **Production Waves** (not phases)\n- **GitHub is the studio** - all work happens here\n- Documentation linked to every issue\n- Clear progression from invisible → visible → alive → consequential → autonomous → multiplayer → beautiful\n\n---\n\n## Production Waves\n\nRepublic develops in 7 Production Waves:\n\n```\nWAVE 0: FOUNDATION\n  → Build nothing visible. Only architecture.\n  → Milestone: \"The skeleton works\"\n  → Duration: 2-3 weeks\n\nWAVE 1: SIMULATION CORE\n  → Build the world without presentation.\n  → Milestone: \"The world lives without a player\"\n  → Duration: 3-4 weeks\n\nWAVE 2: DECISION INTERFACE\n  → Build how players make decisions.\n  → Milestone: \"The player has an office\"\n  → Duration: 4-6 weeks\n\nWAVE 3: EMERGENCE & DEPTH\n  → Build interconnected consequences.\n  → Milestone: \"Every choice matters and echoes\"\n  → Duration: 6-8 weeks\n\nWAVE 4: INTELLIGENCE & AUTONOMY\n  → Build thinking players (AI).\n  → Milestone: \"The world doesn't need you to survive\"\n  → Duration: 4-6 weeks\n\nWAVE 5: MULTIPLAYER & EMERGENCE\n  → Build multiplayer politics.\n  → Milestone: \"Politics becomes personal\"\n  → Duration: 4-6 weeks\n\nWAVE 6: PRESENTATION & POLISH\n  → Build what you see.\n  → Milestone: \"The world looks like leadership\"\n  → Duration: 4-6 weeks\n```\n\n**Total Timeline**: ~39 weeks (9 months)\n\n---\n\n## GitHub Structure\n\n### Issues\n✅ Every task is an issue\n✅ Tagged with Wave label (Wave-0-Foundation, etc)\n✅ Assigned to appropriate Wave milestone\n✅ Linked to design documentation\n✅ Linked to project board\n\n### Project Boards (8 total)\n✅ **Wave 0 - Foundation** board\n✅ **Wave 1 - Simulation Core** board\n✅ **Wave 2 - Decision Interface** board\n✅ **Wave 3 - Emergence & Depth** board\n✅ **Wave 4 - Intelligence & Autonomy** board\n✅ **Wave 5 - Multiplayer & Emergence** board\n✅ **Wave 6 - Presentation & Polish** board\n✅ **Active Sprint** (master board) - real-time view across all Waves\n\nEach board has columns:\n- **Backlog** - not assigned to sprint\n- **Not Started** - ready to begin\n- **In Progress** - actively being worked on\n- **In Review** - PR submitted, testing phase\n- **Done** - complete and merged\n\n### Milestones (7 total)\n✅ One milestone per Wave\n✅ Represents Wave completion gate\n✅ Has due date\n✅ Issues assigned to milestone\n✅ Closed when all issues done\n\n### Labels\n✅ Wave labels (Wave-0-Foundation, etc)\n✅ Priority labels (Critical, High, Medium, Low)\n✅ Type labels (epic, feature, bug, chore, docs)\n✅ Status labels (blocked, in-progress, ready-review, done)\n\n### Discussions\n✅ Design debates before implementation\n✅ Feature proposals\n✅ Questions and answers\n✅ Team announcements\n✅ Post-mortems and learnings\n\n### Documentation\n✅ [PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md) - Development strategy\n✅ [REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md) - Setup guide\n✅ [Roadmap](../Docs/Roadmap/README.md) - Wave timeline and status\n✅ [Features](../Docs/Features/README.md) - What to build per Wave\n✅ [Design Principles](../Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/Design%20Principles.md) - Republic Test\n✅ [Republic Manifesto](../Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/MANIFESTO.md) - The north star\n\n---\n\n## How to Use Republic Studio\n\n### New Team Member\n1. Read [Republic Manifesto](../Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/MANIFESTO.md) (2 min)\n2. Read [Design Principles](../Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/Design%20Principles.md) (10 min)\n3. Check [Roadmap](../Docs/Roadmap/README.md) to see current Wave\n4. Review current Wave [project board](https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game/projects)\n5. Pick an issue labeled with current Wave\n6. Move it to \"In Progress\"\n7. Start building!\n\n### Daily Developer Workflow\n1. **Morning**: Check [Active Sprint board](https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game/projects)\n2. **Pick issue**: From current Wave board (in \"Not Started\")\n3. **Move to \"In Progress\"**: Mark issue as yours\n4. **Build**: Code per specifications\n5. **Create PR**: Link to issue (auto-closes when merged)\n6. **Move to \"In Review\"**: Issue auto-moves when PR created\n7. **Move to \"Done\"**: Issue auto-moves when PR merged\n8. **Repeat**: Pick next issue\n\n### Weekly Sprint Planning\n1. Open current Wave project board\n2. Review Backlog column\n3. Move ready issues to \"Not Started\"\n4. Set Priority and Story Points\n5. Assign developers\n6. Check velocity from last sprint\n7. Commit to sprint\n\n### Wave Completion\n1. Move all issues to \"Done\"\n2. Achieve Wave milestone\n3. Close milestone\n4. Document completion\n5. Begin next Wave\n\n---\n\n## Key Documents\n\n### Strategy\n- **[PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md)** - Complete Wave strategy and philosophy\n- **[Republic Manifesto](../Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/MANIFESTO.md)** - \"Republic is not a game about ruling people. It is a game about carrying the burden of democratic leadership.\"\n- **[Design Principles](../Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/Design%20Principles.md)** - 16 core principles + the Republic Test\n\n### Development\n- **[REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md)** - How to set up GitHub as Republic Studio\n- **[Roadmap](../Docs/Roadmap/README.md)** - Wave timeline, progress, and completion gates\n- **[Features](../Docs/Features/README.md)** - What systems to build in each Wave\n- **[Architecture](../Docs/Architecture/README.md)** - How to build them\n\n### Player Experience\n- **[Gameplay Philosophy](../Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/README.md)** - Design vision and pillars\n- **[UX](../Docs/UX/README.md)** - Interface and player interaction patterns\n\n---\n\n## The Wave Progression\n\n### Wave 0: Foundation (Weeks 1-3)\n**Objective**: Build nothing visible. Only architecture.\n\n**What gets built**:\n- Project Bootstrap & Build Pipeline\n- Logging System\n- Event Bus\n- Time Engine\n- Simulation Core\n- Dependency Injection\n- Game Loop\n- Configuration Manager\n- Save System (framework)\n- Folder Structure\n\n**What players see**: Nothing. Zero UI.\n\n**Milestone**: \"The skeleton works\" ✓\n\n---\n\n### Wave 1: Simulation Core (Weeks 4-7)\n**Objective**: Build the world without presentation.\n\n**What gets built**:\n- World Manager\n- Country Entity\n- Geography System\n- Population System\n- Resources System\n- Currency System\n- Economic Engine\n- Political System\n- Time-Driven Events\n- System Interconnections\n\n**What players see**: Nothing. Simulation runs without them.\n\n**Milestone**: \"The world lives without a player\" ✓\n\n---\n\n### Wave 2: Decision Interface (Weeks 8-13)\n**Objective**: Build how players make decisions.\n\n**What gets built**:\n- Executive Workspace\n- Decision Spaces\n- Advisor System\n- Cabinet Interface\n- Reports System\n- Communication System\n- Dashboards\n- Briefing Rooms\n- Notifications\n- Save/Load UI\n\n**What players see**: An office. Ministers bringing proposals. Ability to decide.\n\n**Milestone**: \"The player has an office\" ✓\n\n---\n\n### Wave 3: Emergence & Depth (Weeks 14-21)\n**Objective**: Build interconnected consequences.\n\n**What gets built**:\n- Legislative System\n- Faction System\n- Legitimacy System\n- Diplomatic Relations\n- Military System\n- Trade Networks\n- Crisis System\n- Succession System\n- Constitutional System\n- Media System\n\n**What players see**: Consequences cascade. Every choice has winners and losers.\n\n**Milestone**: \"Every choice matters and echoes\" ✓\n\n---\n\n### Wave 4: Intelligence & Autonomy (Weeks 22-27)\n**Objective**: Build thinking players (AI).\n\n**What gets built**:\n- AI Decision Engine\n- Economic AI\n- Political AI\n- Military AI\n- Diplomatic AI\n- Advisor Personalities\n- Opposition System\n- International Crises\n- Adaptive Difficulty\n- Emergent Storytelling\n\n**What players see**: World acts without them. Political intrigue happens. Nations pursue agendas.\n\n**Milestone**: \"The world doesn't need you to survive\" ✓\n\n---\n\n### Wave 5: Multiplayer & Emergence (Weeks 28-33)\n**Objective**: Build multiplayer politics.\n\n**What gets built**:\n- Network Protocol\n- Server Architecture\n- State Synchronization\n- Player Sessions\n- Conflict Resolution\n- Emergent Diplomacy\n- Alliance System\n- Trading System\n- Espionage System\n- Co-op/Competitive Modes\n\n**What players see**: Other players! Political intrigue becomes personal. Alliances form and break.\n\n**Milestone**: \"Politics becomes personal\" ✓\n\n---\n\n### Wave 6: Presentation & Polish (Weeks 34-39)\n**Objective**: Build what you see.\n\n**What gets built**:\n- 3D Office Environment\n- Character Animation\n- Visual Effects\n- Audio System\n- Cinematics\n- Tutorial System\n- Accessibility Features\n- Performance Optimization\n- Quality Assurance\n- Release Polish\n\n**What players see**: Beautiful, immersive, polished game.\n\n**Milestone**: \"The world looks like leadership\" ✓ **RELEASE READY**\n\n---\n\n## Success Metrics\n\n**Republic Studio is working when:**\n\n✅ All Wave work visible on project boards\n✅ Issues move across columns as work progresses\n✅ Developers update issues daily\n✅ Issues linked to design documentation\n✅ Velocity tracked and trending\n✅ Blockers identified and escalated\n✅ Wave milestones achieved on schedule\n✅ Team communicates via GitHub (no external chat)\n✅ Everything lives in GitHub (one source of truth)\n✅ Wave completion gates achieved\n✅ Next Wave starts immediately after current\n✅ Momentum builds through all 6 Waves\n\n---\n\n## Republic Studio Philosophy\n\n### One Source of Truth\nEverything lives in GitHub:\n- Strategy\n- Design\n- Implementation\n- Testing\n- Deployment\n- Team communication\n\nNo external tools. No scattered discussions. One studio.\n\n### Clear Wave Progression\nEach Wave:\n- Has clear objective\n- Builds on previous Waves\n- Delivers specific systems\n- Achieves milestone gate\n- Enables next Wave\n\nNo skipping. No shortcuts. Each Wave complete before next.\n\n### Visible Progress\nEveryone can see:\n- Current Wave status\n- What's in progress\n- What's blocked\n- When Wave completes\n- Path to release\n\nNo surprises. No hidden work. Transparent progress.\n\n### Connected Documentation\nEvery issue connected to:\n- Design principles it serves\n- Wave it belongs to\n- Acceptance criteria\n- Technical specifications\n- Implementation notes\n\nNo orphaned issues. No forgotten requirements. All documented.\n\n---\n\n## Getting Started\n\n1. ✅ **Read** [PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md)\n2. ✅ **Understand** [Republic Manifesto](../Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/MANIFESTO.md)\n3. ✅ **Study** [Design Principles](../Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/Design%20Principles.md)\n4. ✅ **Check** [Roadmap](../Docs/Roadmap/README.md) for current Wave\n5. ✅ **Follow** [REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md) to set up GitHub\n6. ✅ **Open** current [Wave project board](https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game/projects)\n7. ✅ **Pick** an issue\n8. ✅ **Build** Republic\n\n---\n\n## Timeline\n\n| Week | Wave | Status | Milestone |\n|------|------|--------|----------|\n| 1-3 | Wave 0 | 🟡 Current | \"The skeleton works\" |\n| 4-7 | Wave 1 | 🔴 Next | \"The world lives without a player\" |\n| 8-13 | Wave 2 | 🔴 Planned | \"The player has an office\" |\n| 14-21 | Wave 3 | 🔴 Planned | \"Every choice matters and echoes\" |\n| 22-27 | Wave 4 | 🔴 Planned | \"The world doesn't need you to survive\" |\n| 28-33 | Wave 5 | 🔴 Planned | \"Politics becomes personal\" |\n| 34-39 | Wave 6 | 🔴 Planned | \"The world looks like leadership\" |\n| **39** | **COMPLETE** | ✅ | **RELEASE READY** |\n\n---\n\n## Links\n\n### Core Strategy\n- [PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md)\n- [Republic Manifesto](../Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/MANIFESTO.md)\n- [Design [Features](../Docs/Features/README.md)\n\n### GitHub\n- [Repository](https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game)\n- [Issues](https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game/issues)\n- [Projects](https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game/projects)\n- [Milestones](https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game/milestones)\n- [Discussions](https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game/discussions)\n\n---\n\n*Republic is built in Production Waves. GitHub is the studio. This is how we ship.*\n"
